channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: First Two Wrestlers Set for WWE 2K26 Ringside Pass Season 3
item.content:encoded:
The first two WWE Superstars have been announced for the third season of the WWE 2K26 Ringside Pass. One is a freshly returned WWE Superstar, and the other is a legend who has held numerous titles.

Matt Cardona, formerly known as Zack Ryder, is making his return to WWE 2K games as part of the third season of the Ringside Pass. He announced his return in a video on social media. The last time Cardona was in a game was WWE 2K20 in 2019. Cardona was released by WWE in 2020 and recently returned to WWE.

While he was away, he was a playable character in the game Retromania Wrestling.

WWE 2K26 Will Feature A ‘Loose Cannon’ As Well

In addition to Matt Cardona, WWE 2K26 Ringside Pass Season 3 will also include the legendary Brian Pillman. Pillman’s inclusion was announced by his son, current WWE NXT Superstar Lexis King. King mentioned that he was excited for players to play as both him and his father, and hopefully make them a tag team.

Brian Pillman hasn’t been featured in a WWE video game in 10 years. His last appearance was in WWE 2K17. Pillman, who passed away in 1997, made his video game debut in WCW Superbrawl on the Super Nintendo back in 1994. He would also be featured in the 2000s Legends of Wrestling video game series.

WWE 2K26 Sees the Return of Demolition As Well

In addition to these announcements, it was revealed during the most recent episode of Monday Night Raw that Ax and Smash, Demolition, would be featured in the second season of the WWE 2K26 Ringside Pass. This will be the first time Ax and Smash have been featured in a game since WWE ’12 in 2011.

Like Cardona, Ax, and Smash are in Retromania Wrestling. That game is available now on PlayStation, Xbox, and Nintendo Switch consoles. It’s also available on PC via Steam.

According to Visual Concepts, Season 2 of the Ringside Pass will be available in April before WrestleMania 42, which begins on April 18. Season 3 of the Ringside Pass will be available in June. Learn more about the choice behind the new DLC system at this link.

WWE 2K26 hits Early Access on March 6 for those who purchased premium editions. It will release globally on March 13.

Will you be playing WWE 2K26? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: Build a Rocket Boy Announces More Layoffs, Blames Espionage… Again
item.content:encoded:
Build a Rocket Boy has, unfortunately, announced more layoffs, a decision the Co-CEO is calling “deeply painful.”

Build a Rocket Boy, known for releasing MindsEye, announced the news on LinkedIn. They shared a message from co-CEO Mark Gerhard, in which he praised those who were unfortunately affected by the redundancies, calling them enormously talented.

MindsEye Co-CEO Feels Organized Espionage Hurt MindsEye’s Launch

He also went on to double down on his belief that “organized espionage” and corporate sabotage affected MindsEye, saying they have been working with external partners and legal advisors to investigate the “criminal activity” surrounding the launch.

“Because this matter is moving towards prosecution, we cannot share the full details publicly yet,” the statement reads, before noting that the team has continued working to evolve MindsEye, and that he’s part of the work they’ve done. Still, the prolonged impact of the difficult launch has resulted in further layoffs.

The statement ends by thanking those affected by the news and promising to support them in finding their next opportunity.

In early February, Insider Gaming reported that Gerhard told employees they had caught the perpetrators responsible for the sabotage and noted that the company behind the campaign was a “very big American company” that had allegedly spent over €1 million on a smear campaign against the game in 2025.

Layoffs And Server Shutdowns Remain a Top Gaming Story in 2026

Recently, Wildlight Entertainment announced it would shut down the Highguard servers on March 12. That means the game, which received heavy press and backlash for being the final game featured at the Game Awards in 2025, will go offline after less than 2 months since its launch. Learn more about that decision here.

If you’re curious and want to know more about MindsEye, check out our full review of the game. How do you feel about this news coming out of Build a Rocket Boy? What are your thoughts on MindsEye? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#998)—Answers for Thursday, March 5
item.content:encoded:
You’re more than halfway through the week and just a couple of puzzles away from that major milestone of 1000 NYT Connections puzzles! Are you as excited as I am to see what the answers will be on that all-important day? For today, you’ve got a relatively run-of-the-mill challenge to dominate, and that’s where I come in.

Scroll down to get today’s NYT Connections hints and solutions in just a few seconds.

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (March 5)

Here are the words from today’s NYT Connections puzzle:

  • ALTERNATE
  • HERO
  • TOGGLE
  • BOLT
  • REPLACEMENT
  • SANDBOX
  • BELOW
  • SEESAW
  • NUT
  • LAB
  • WASHER
  • SUBMARINE
  • INCUBATOR
  • SWITCH
  • SCREW
  • TEST BED

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Items you might use when building a cabinet
  • Green – Places and tools you’d use to develop an idea
  • Blue – Words you might associate with indecision
  • Purple – Words that can be linked to the term ‘sub’

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, March 5:

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, March 5 (#998)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – BOLT, NUT, SCREW, WASHER
  • Green – INCUBATOR, LAB, SANDBOX, TEST BED
  • Blue – ALTERNATE, SEESAW, SWITCH, TOGGLE
  • Purple – BELOW, HERO, REPLACEMENT, SUBMARINE

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill
